summary: Iteration 65 - Payment Migration (Destination Charges → Separate Charges & Transfers) testing complete. All 21 backend tests PASSED. Key verification: create-checkout and buy-now NO LONGER use payment_intent_data.transfer_data or application_fee_amount - they now use transfer_group only. Webhook processes checkout.session.completed and triggers execute_seller_transfers() and schedule_influencer_payout(). New admin endpoints verified: POST /payments/process-influencer-payouts and GET /payments/scheduled-payouts with proper role-based access control.
passed_tests: GET /api/producer/payments - PASS (200, all required fields present)
POST /api/payments/create-checkout (with cart) - PASS (200, Stripe session returned)
POST /api/checkout/buy-now (valid product) - PASS (200, Stripe session returned)
critical_code_review_comments: Payment architecture correctly migrated to Separate Charges & Transfers
No destination charges (transfer_data/application_fee_amount) in checkout sessions
transfer_group correctly links payments to transfers for reconciliation
Idempotency keys prevent duplicate transfers on retry
transfers_executed flag provides additional idempotency at order level
Webhook is primary handler with checkout-status as fallback
Admin endpoints properly protected with role-based access
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 65 verified migration from Stripe Destination Charges to Separate Charges & Transfers. Key changes: 1) create-checkout and buy-now use payment_intent_data.transfer_group (NOT transfer_data), 2) Webhook processes checkout.session.completed and calls process_payment_confirmed which executes seller transfers and schedules influencer payouts, 3) checkout-status acts as fallback, 4) New admin endpoints for scheduled payouts management. All role-based access controls working. STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET can be empty for dev/test mode (skips signature verification).
mocked_api_notes: No mocked APIs - all features use real backend implementation with Stripe SDK
